scope: ~/Projects/Gnu.in/ — all repos, read-only design & spec audit
intent: Audit the workspace for deprecated data, misalignment, and out-of-date specs across every repo's docs, contracts, assets, and components. Deliver a findings register, a priority-ordered remediation work order, and paste-ready fix drafts. Re-framed against the decision that QML surfaces are retired-as-tech: the forward chrome is native Rust/sctk under gnu.in-os/engine/gnuin-*, so QML-surface fixes are interim or wontfix and the real debt is docs/contracts not catching up to the v0.14.x native migration.
render_note: The .dc.html artifacts load ./support.js (this folder) which fetches React from a CDN. Open over a local web server with network access, OR drop the four .dc.html files at the real repo root to use its existing support.js/dc-boot.js. The Markdown files need no runtime.

files.reason: Handoff orientation: what this is, how to open the interactive artifacts, the artifact index, and the do-first list.
The full findings register: 26 entries (F01–F26) with severity, evidence, file paths, impact, and fix, plus carried-forward open items. Portable/greppable source of truth.
22 atomic remediation tasks (T01–T22), priority-ordered (P0–P4), with verify conditions and draft cross-refs. Markdown mirror of the interactive checklist.
Interactive findings report — severity-coded cards with evidence blocks and a remediation-priority table.

Interactive 22-task work order. Check-off state persists in localStorage (key gnuin-audit-checklist-v2); progress bar; QML-retirement banner + per-task notes; tweak toggles (hide completed / show verify / show change).

Paste-ready before/after blocks for the P0 + P1 doc edits (12 blocks) with copy buttons. Covers SKILL.md x5, WORKSPACE_INVENTORY, README x3, DESIGN_ASSET_MANIFEST, DESIGN_SOURCE_INVENTORY, and the Lift/Origami close-out.

Contract/schema drafts + decision memo: surfaces.toml migration-state keys + topbar->taskbar rename (with a 19-surface mapping table), runtime.toml pointer fixes, the chrome icon-family decision (3 options + recommendation), and the launcher-badge code pointer.
DC runtime, copied from repo root so the .dc.html files render standalone. Unchanged.

key_findings: F01/F02 CRITICAL — WORKSPACE_INVENTORY states v0.12.0 (actual v0.14.2) and 4 stale component revs, one on the wrong branch.

F03/F04/F05/F07/F08 CRITICAL/WARNING — SKILL.md (agent entry point) contradicts the live token authority on chrome kit, motion layer, mascot asset, canonical surface (#F5EEDD vs #F7F3ED), and token version.
F06 CRITICAL — error.log at workspace root leaks a live account ID.
F13/F17 — surfaces.toml lags the engine (native hosts unrecorded) and uses topbar where the surface is taskbar.
F15 DECISION — chrome icon family: native bar ships Nerd-Font MDI; design system mandates Material Symbols Rounded.
F26 RESOLVED — the 2026-06-11 taskbar clock-clip is already fixed in Lift.qml/Origami.qml; the audit item was never closed.
decisions_pending: T11 — chrome icon family (recommendation: split by layer; native chrome = Nerd-Font MDI).
T09 — topbar->taskbar rename scope sign-off (decided: standardize on taskbar).
F05 — align docs to the #F7F3ED token, or change the token to #F5EEDD (must match).
not_in_drop: No repo source was modified — this is a read-only audit deliverable. The fixes are drafted, not applied. The wider design-system corpus (Atlas/Fondations/Atomes/etc.) and the gnu-in-shell-docs-major-drop are unrelated and excluded.
